source_citation: OurWorldinData.org/life-expectancy | CC BY. Data source: Riley (2005); Zijdeman et al. (2015); HMD (2025); UN WPP (2024).
subtitle_verbatim: Period life expectancy is the number of years the average person born in a certain year would live if they experienced the same chances of dying at each age as people did that year.
notes: Our World in Data line chart, 6 regional series. Legend labels appear at the right end of each line (not in a box) with L-shaped gray connectors leading to color-matched text. Y-axis labels formatted as "NN years" (0 to 80 in steps of 10). X-axis labels: 1770, 1800, 1850, 1900, 1950, 2000, 2023 (endpoint labels nudged inward by renderer).
